description: Monetary notional amount effective for leg 1 on a specific date.
Notional amount for leg 1 (initial or current outstanding); rules for schedules, amendments, FX options, and CDS gross notional apply

For Leg 2, the contractual notional amount; initial notional at inception, updated for lifecycle events to the outstanding amount; report full terminated value if fully terminated; FX options also report Call and Put amounts; CDS notional reflects gross
Price specified in the OTC derivative transaction (excluding fees, taxes, commissions); initial or forward price depending on product; updated when available
Aggregate notional quantity of the underlying asset for Leg 1 over the term of the transaction, updated when available
Monetary amount the option gives the right to buy for the first leg.
Number of time units determining payment frequency for the fixed rate on leg 1 (paired with payment period; special cases for ADHO, EXPI, DAIL)
Number of time units (per the payment frequency period) determining how often floating-leg payments occur for leg 1; special cases: ADHO not applicable, EXPI=1, intraday uses DAIL with multiplier 0
Number of time units for the reset frequency of the floating leg for the first leg.
Non-monetary quantity specified for the first leg of the contract.
Monetary amount the option gives the right to sell for the first leg.
Number of time units for the quantity frequency for the first leg.
Manner in which the spread for the first leg is expressed.
Spread on the floating leg index reference price for the first leg.
Aggregate notional quantity of the underlying for leg 2 over the transaction’s term; updated when available
Monetary amount the option gives the right to buy for the second leg.
Number of time units for the fixed rate payment frequency for the second leg.

Number of time units (as defined by the payment frequency period) that determines how often payments occur for the floating-rate leg on Leg 2; not applicable if ADHO, equals 1 if EXPI, and equals 0 if intraday (DAIL)

Number of time units (as defined by the floating rate reset frequency period) that determines how often resets occur on Leg 2; not applicable if ADHO, equals 1 if EXPI, and equals 0 if intraday (DAIL)
Non-monetary quantity specified for the second leg of the contract.
Notional amount effective on a specific date for the second leg.
Monetary amount the option gives the right to sell for the second leg.
Number of time units for the quantity frequency for the second leg.
Manner in which the spread for the second leg is expressed.
Spread on the floating leg index reference price for the second leg.
Amount paid for the option premium.
Manner in which the strike price is expressed for the option.
Price at which the owner of an option can buy or sell the underlying asset.
Date and time the transaction was originally executed, creating the UTI; remains unchanged for the life of the UTI. For cleared swaps, it is when the DCO accepts the original swap
Specifies the manner in which the price of a package transaction is expressed.
Manner in which the price is expressed.
Rate at which two currencies are exchanged in the transaction.
